business.headline: Markets end lower as crude stays high, rupee hits record lowMarkets closed lower on Thursday as high crude prices and a weak rupee weighed on sentiment. The slide highlighted persistent inflation and capital flow concerns, even as IT and pharma offered limited support.

Bajaj Finserv announces dividend, reports strong FY26 growthBajaj Finserv reported record FY26 income and profit and announced a special dividend with its Q4 results. The update also marked the completion of Allianz's stake exit, leaving its insurance businesses fully Bajaj-owned.

India-NZ FTA opens doors, but only prepared firms will benefitSiddharth Shankar says the India-New Zealand FTA offers big openings for trade and talent. He argues only firms with strong systems, compliance and execution will truly benefit.

8th Pay Commission: What worker bodies have demanded ahead of salary reviewWorker representatives laid out pay, pension and service demands in the first 8th Pay Commission meeting in Delhi. The staff side said the talks ended positively but pressed for wider consultation and more meetings.

OnEMI Technology IPO: Attractive or risky for long-term investors?Founded in 2016, OnEMI operates in the fast-growing digital lending space. It offers personal loans and loans against property with quick approvals and minimal paperwork—something that has clearly clicked with young borrowers.

Where should a 25-year-old invest to build wealth: SIPs, stocks or crypto?At 25, with time on your side, what's the smartest way to invest, i.e., SIPs (Systematic Investment Plans), stocks or crypto? Let's have a look.
Big EPFO update: Minimum pension may rise 7.5 times, ATM withdrawals soonThe government is mulling a sharp increase in the minimum pension under the Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS-95), say sources.

From TDS mismatches to capital gains errors: 3 risks under Income Tax Act 2025The Income Tax Act, 2025 is now in force, and for many companies, the transition seems smooth so far. But could small gaps like TDS mismatches and capital gains errors turn into bigger compliance issues?

Rupee sinks to record low as oil spike and Fed stance pile on pressureThe rupee slipped to a record low against the US dollar as crude oil prices surged and the Federal Reserve stayed hawkish. The move underlines pressure from India's rising import bill, firmer dollar demand and tighter global financial conditions.

Sensex down 400 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The BSE Sensex was down 1,215.91 points, or 1.54%, at 76,280.45 around 10:58 am, while the Nifty 50 fell 366.70 points, or 1.51%, to 23,811.25.
Sensex opens 700 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; IndiGo down 2%The &P BSE Sensex was down 947.08 points to 76,549.28, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 317.60 points to 23,860.05 as of 10:01 am.

The rapid switch, later detailed by the Financial Times, sharpened attention on his exit and his rising clout at Citi.

Infosys won't cut jobs despite AI shift, says CEO Salil ParekhInfosys CEO Salil Parekh said the company does not plan layoffs despite the AI shift. The stance sets it apart from peers and underscores a hiring-and-reskilling push.

Crude oil tops $120, highest since 2022: What it means for markets and rupeeDisruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil transit route, have added to the uncertainty, taking crude oil prices to their highest levels since mid-2022.

8th Pay Commission update: Has the last date been extended amid employee demands?The Commission is holding a three-day consultation session from April 28 to April 30. During these meetings, representatives from various employee groups are discussing key issues related to pay, allowances and service conditions.

Rajiv Bajaj to step down from Bajaj Finance board at end of termRajiv Bajaj's tenure as a non-executive director will conclude at the close of the company's upcoming annual general meeting, in line with regulatory requirements.

Odisha bank case explained: Rules for withdrawing money after deathOnce an account holder dies, the account is no longer treated as a normal account. Any access to funds becomes a claim settlement process, not a simple withdrawal.
Sensex, Nifty end higher as Q4 earnings boost outweighs crude oil surgeSensex ends 609 points higher, Nifty tops 24,100; RIL up 3%

Kissht IPO opens April 30: Price band, GMP, lot size and key detailsThe Rs 925.92 crore IPO will open on April 30 and close on May 5. The allotment is likely to be finalised on May 6, while listing on the BSE and NSE is expected on May 8.

entertainment.headline: Kara review: Dhanush's heist film knows the drill, but misses the jackpotRelease Date:Thu Apr 30, 2026 Kara movie review: Director Vignesh Raja's Kara, starring Dhanush, Mamitha Baiju and Suraj Venjaramoodu, is a film about a robber caught in a life-altering situation that pulls him back into crime. Yami Gautam says Aditya is unaffected by Dhurandhar success Yami Gautam, in an interview, said that Dhurandhar's success has not changed Aditya Dhar or their lives. She said the film's run shows audiences are backing strong scripts and returning to cinemas.

Raja Shivaji eyes biggest Marathi opening, advance booking crosses Rs 2.5 crore Riteish Deshmukh's Raja Shivaji is poised to post the biggest opening ever for a Marathi film on May 1. Strong bookings from Maharashtra could push the historical drama past existing records.

Release Dhruva Natchathiram on or before June 15: Court clears Vikram's film The Madras High Court has laid down a framework to enable Dhruva Natchathiram's release by June 15. The order puts financial oversight in place while pending dues and exhibitor talks continue.

Michael OTT release: When and where to watch Michael Jackson's biopic film Michael has opened strongly in cinemas as viewers now wait for its home release. The biopic is expected on Prime Video soon after its theatrical run.

Watch: Kiara's video singing Baby Shark on Maldives trip leaves Sidharth amused Siddharth Malhotra turned cameraman as Kiara Advani sang the children's song Baby Shark. The clip, which is now going viral, stood out among the couple's family holiday posts from the Maldives.

No one uses their sattar minute on the global stage better than Diljit Dosanjh Diljit Dosanjh drops history on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon while Bollywood sticks to thank-you speeches. This is how you use a global mic right!

Still friends? The episode comes after Samay's Still Alive set, where he revisited the India's Got Latent controversy involving Ranveer.

Queen Camilla meets Carrie Bradshaw: A royal Sex and the City moment in NYC From Sex and the City drama to a royal literary moment, the New York Public Library had a real-life crossover. Carrie Bradshaw's iconic wedding backdrop got its most unexpected guest yet — Queen Camilla.

Khatron Ke Khiladi 15: Avinash Mishra, Avika Gor and Rithvik Dhanjani confirmed Avinash Mishra, Avika Gor and Rithvik Dhanjani have been confirmed for Khatron Ke Khiladi 15. The new season, returning after a year-long gap, will begin filming next month.

Rishi Kapoor death anniversary: Neetu, Riddhima and others share heartfelt tribute Actor Neetu Kapoor remembered her husband, actor Rishi Kapoor, on his sixth death anniversary with a heartfelt post. Their daughter Riddhima Kapoor Sahni and others also paid tribute.

Katrina Kaif gives a 'dad' twist to Vicky Kaushal promoting her makeup brand Vicky Kaushal playfully promoted a beauty product from wife Katrina Kaif's makeup brand on Instagram, prompting a witty reply from her. Their exchange blended humour, new-parent life and the easy warmth fans rarely see online.

Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas marries Kavya Reddy in intimate Tirupati temple wedding Tollywood actor Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas married Kavya Reddy in a traditional late-night ceremony at Tirupati's Sri Venkateswara Temple, surrounded by family and friends.

Through love, hope, and surrogacy: Actor Sambhavna Seth announces pregnancy Actor Sambhavna Seth and her husband, actor-writer Avinash Dwivedi, have announced they are expecting their first child through surrogacy. The post comes months after the couple spoke about a miscarriage and several failed IVF attempts.

Exclusive: BTS' long-time producer Pdogg on ARIRANG, BTS 2.0, Grammys In a rare exclusive interview with India Today, BTS' long-time producer Pdogg talks ARIRANG, the group's new era, solo journeys and evolving sound. He also spoke about Grammys ambition.

Rahul Roy reacts to trolls over viral reels: Help me find work if you are concerned Actor Rahul Roy has hinted at financial struggles while responding to the trolling around his recent viral reels. Saying that the reaction was hurtful, he said if people were really concerned, they could help him find decent work to pay his legal bills.

RAFA: Netflix set to serve Rafael Nadal documentary. See premiere date, new poster Netflix has unveiled new posters for RAFA, its documentary series on Rafael Nadal, ahead of the May 29 premiere. The visuals frame the series around endurance, injury, fatherhood and the final stretch of his career.

general news.headline: The secret weapon that Iran says can give US a 'heart attack'Iran has hinted at unveiling a new weapon, which it said might give its enemies a 'heart attack', as talks with the US have hit a dead end. Experts have hinted that it might be the Hoot super torpedo that can slice through warships in no time.

AIADMK eyes talks with Vijay's TVK after exit poll hints strong debut in Tamil NaduAIADMK is exploring alliance talks with Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam following exit polls projecting TVK's strong debut in Tamil Nadu elections. Vijay also leads as the most preferred Chief Minister candidate, with notable support from young and diverse voter groups.

India pulls off a power miracle. A big thanks to the SunIndia touched an all-time peak power demand without any shortage, and it was solar that stepped up at the most critical hour, supplying nearly 21% of the electricity. This moment underscores a quiet but decisive shift that the sun is no longer just a supplement but a key driver of India's energy resilience.

BRICS at risk of imploding, reveals Delhi meetThere is a wide rift in the BRICS, of which India is a founding member, due to the war in the Middle East. There was no joint statement after the recent meeting in New Delhi that was described as "very tense". India as the BRICS president will have to do a tight rope walk ahead of the crucial summit in September.

CBSE parenting calendar triggers debate on schools guiding parentsCBSE may call it a partnership, but for many parents, this feels like something new; a system that doesn't just teach children, but quietly begins to teach parents how to raise them.

Rain, hail hits Delhi-NCR: Orange alert issued, weather system to worsenThe dual impact of cooling rains and gusty winds is expected to bring daytime temperatures down noticeably in affected areas, offering a temporary break before conditions potentially stabilise again.

No need to humiliate me with arrest, Pawan Khera tells SC at bail hearingThe Supreme Court heard Pawan Khera's anticipatory bail plea in a defamation case, with his lawyer Abhishek Manu Singhvi accusing Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma of bias and calling his remarks "constitutional cowboy."

Godrej Industries lays out 2031 growth ambitionsThe purpose statement, 'Crafting tomorrow since 1897', celebrates the Godrej Group's legacy while clearly positioning the new, refreshed Godrej Industries for the future.

Deceived, used: US journalist as Pak event turns into govt-bashing showAn informal media gathering in Pakistan involving foreign reporters, who were stationed for the US-Iran talks, turned into a full-blown controversy. What was supposed to be a meet-and-greet event turned into a political spectacle after two Pakistani journalists used the platform to criticise the government and the military.

Hope they don't have a heart attack: Iran teases US with new weapon as talks stallAs deadlocked talks between the United States and Iran intensify tensions, Tehran has warned it will soon unveil a weapon its enemies may 'get a heart attack' over.

Watch: Venkatesh Prasad's child-like joy in Bengaluru hailstorm, tastes it tooFormer India cricketer Venkatesh Prasad stopped his car during Bengaluru's sudden hailstorm and shared a playful video online. The clip struck a chord as the rare spell cooled the city and briefly turned parts of it white.

Live: Israel orders 8 Lebanese towns' residents to leave homes before strikesUS-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has rejected Iran's proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift the blockade, choosing to tighten pressure instead of easing it. He argued the blockade is more effective than military strikes, signalling that Washington is relying on economic strain rather than immediate escalation to force Tehran's hand. That pressure is already showing. Iran's currency has slipped to a record low of 1,810,000 rials to the dollar, according to state-linked media, highlighting the growing toll on its economy. Behind the scenes, the US appears to be settling in for a longer standoff. Trump and his aides have held talks with energy executives on how to keep the blockade in place for months while trying to shield American consumers from rising costs. The political heat is rising at home as well. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth defended the war during a congressional hearing, brushing aside criticism and calling opponents "the biggest adversary" as he faced lawmakers for the first time since the conflict began. Diplomacy, meanwhile, remains stuck. Pakistan is expected to receive a revised Iranian proposal, but Trump has shown little patience, warning Tehran to "better get smart soon." He also spoke with Russian President Vladimir Putin, who offered to help address Iran's uranium stockpiles, adding another layer to the ongoing negotiations. At the same time, Israel has launched fresh strikes in southern Lebanon, killing emergency workers and soldiers. Israeli forces have made it clear there is "no ceasefire" in their campaign against Hezbollah, despite ongoing efforts for direct talks. Catch all the live updates at indiatoday.in.

No OTP, no problem: Ahmedabad gang uses AI deepfakes to bypass Aadhaar checksThe case came to light after an Ahmedabad businessman noticed he had stopped receiving OTPs from his bank for two days. He approached the police after noticing something was amiss.

EC gets 77 EVM tampering complaints from Bengal, decision on re-polling todayThe Election Commission will decide on Thursday whether to order re-polling in parts of West Bengal after 77 complaints of alleged EVM tampering.

To find an answer, a US government team started an investigation. That investigation, however, was killed suddenly when one agent reportedly found something.

Poll of polls: DMK in TN, Congress return in Kerala, NDA in Assam, Bengal suspenseA poll of exit polls have predicted a big win for the BJP in Assam and gave the party an edge ahead of the ruling TMC in West Bengal. The polls also suggested that the DMK is likely to return to power in Tamil Nadu, while the Congress-led UDF may make a comeback in Kerala after 10 years.

Relief for Karisma Kapoor's children as Delhi court freezes Sunjay Kapur's assetsThe Delhi High Court, in its order, said that Sunjay Kapur's bank accounts and cryptocurrency holdings will remain non-operational for the time being.

Bali streets piled with trash after island's largest landfill declared off-limitsBali is facing a mounting waste crisis after authorities restricted organic waste at its largest landfill without ready alternatives, leaving garbage piling up in streets, attracting pests and forcing residents to burn it.

1 litre of ethanol needs 10,000 litres water: Hidden cost of India's fuel pushIndia's total ethanol production capacity stands at 1,822 crore litres, with a disproportionate share concentrated in already water-stressed states like Delhi and Maharashtra.
Bengaluru rain: What fuelled the intense hailstorm? Will it happen again?Such pre-monsoon showers are normal for April but serve as a reminder of how quickly weather can change in the city.

I-PAC director gets bail day after Bengal polls as ED does not oppose pleaA day after the conclusion of the West Bengal polls, the director of Trinamool's political strategist consultancy was granted bail after the ED said it would not oppose his plea.

sports.headline: Cristiano Ronaldo five UCL gesture goes viral after Al Ahli fans' title jibe Cristiano Ronaldo answered Al Ahli fans by holding up five fingers after Al Nassr's 2-0 win. The exchange went viral and sharpened the bad blood around a match already clouded by refereeing complaints and on-pitch tension.

Release Hardik Pandya: Mumbai Indians told to reset after 3 mellow years in IPL Simon Doull has urged Mumbai Indians to consider releasing Hardik Pandya if they don't think he will be able to lead the team into the next cycle. Mumbai Indians have had a tough time in IPL 2026. They are rooted to the 9th spot with just two wins in eight games.

Fresh trouble for Riyan Parag? RR captain escapes ban, but women's group seeks probe A women's group has sought an inquiry into visuals purportedly showing Riyan Parag with a vape-like device during an IPL match. It says the incident could normalise a banned product among young viewers and wants accountability.

Riyan Parag escapes ban for vaping during IPL match, fined by BCCI IPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India has fined Riyan Parag 25% of his match fee for vaping during an IPL match in Mullanpur, with the Rajasthan Royals captain escaping a stricter punishment.

The clip struck a chord as the rare spell cooled the city and briefly turned parts of it white.

IPL business first, cricket next? Sri Lanka great's massive claim on run-fests IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad's spin bowling coach Muttiah Muralitharan said flat wickets and the IPL's design are fuelling relentless 200-plus totals. He argued the league prioritises entertainment, sponsors and viewer interest over cricket development after SRH's victory over MI with eight balls to spare.

Travishek Blueprint: The simple secret behind IPL's most destructive opening pair IPL 2026: Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma powered Sunrisers Hyderabad's chase of 245 against Mumbai Indians with another explosive opening stand. Klaasen, has been in a sensational form in the IPL scoring 414 runs at a strike-rate of 15741, placing him second in the Orange Cap race behind teammate Abhishek Sharma.

Champions League: Arteta cries foul over VAR call as Arsenal held by Atletico Champions League semi-final first leg: Coach Mikel Arteta hit out after Arsenal's penalty was overturned by VAR in Madrid. He said the decision denied a potentially decisive win.

His remarks highlighted the scrutiny on cricketers as Bumrah's lean IPL 2026 run continued.

The outing eased fresh health concerns and highlighted the support around the former India batter.

IPL Play of the Day: SRH prove T20 is a bowlers' game too with Wankhede masterclassMI vs SRH, IPL 2026: Sunrisers turned the game at the death, restricting Mumbai Indians to 243 when the home side looked set to cross 260. Ehsan Malinga shone in the final overs, while Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma later laid the foundation for a record chase at Wankhede Stadium on Wednesday.

After teammates, Hardik Pandya snaps at MI fans for supporting opponentsIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya has now turned his focus from teammates to the stands, calling out Mumbai Indians fans for backing opposition teams during this season. The MI captain admitted it "hurts" hearing rival chants at Wankhede after another disappointing home defeat.

No respite for MI as 243 not enough against rampant SRH at WankhedeIPL 2026: SunRisers Hyderabad extended their winning run in the Indian Premier League by recording the 4th highest chase in the history of the tournament. IRGC-linked Iran football body chief lands in Canada, sent back hours laterMehdi Taj, the IRGC-linked president of Iran's football federation, was deported from Canada just hours after arriving for this week's FIFA Congress, reigniting debate over Ottawa's tough stance on figures linked to the Iranian regime.

Ryan Rickelton puts SRH bowlers to sword, hits maiden IPL hundredMI vs SRH: Ryan Rickelton provided Mumbai Indians with the perfect foundation to make a comeback in the Indian Premier League. Playing against SRH, Rickelton hit a 44-ball hundred at the Wankhede Stadium.

Golfer Vijay Kumar, former India Open champion, dies at 57Veteran Indian golfer Vijay Kumar died of a heart attack in Lucknow at 57. The 2002 Indian Open winner and four-time Order of Merit champion leaves behind a lasting legacy in Indian golf.

The government is expected to appoint an interim committee, though the move could draw scrutiny from the ICC given past conflicts over similar interventions.

Badminton Highlights: India finish on a high, Prannoy wins final matchThomas Cup 2026 Highlights, India vs China: India lose to China in the final group-stage tie of the Thomas Cup. HS Prannoy bags a consolation win in the final match of the tie as he beat Lu Guang in a 3-game battle. If India would have shown a little more clinical edge, they might have won this tie and finished top of their group.

FIFA World Cup: Red cards for covering mouth, yellow card suspension rules easedAhead of the FIFA World Cup, the governing body has announced a couple of major changes in rules. The revisions target concealed abuse, protests and time-wasting while reducing yellow-card suspensions in knockout matches.
